wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Mori-universe project overview section focused on the digital archive and analytical framework for Mori Hiroshi's literary works. Explain its purpose as an educational resource mapping the interconnected universe of Japanese author Mori Hiroshi's literary works, particularly the Mori-universe system architecture. Document the implementation details including the tabbed navigation system, color-coded series identification, and interactive visualization capabilities. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers interested in interdisciplinary studies. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including series names (S&M, V, Shiki, G, X, Century, Sky Crawlers, W, WW, Void Shaper). Provide practical examples demonstrating the tab switching functionality, series categorization system, and cross-referencing methodologies. Document the public interfaces including tab navigation, screenshot functionality, and responsive design patterns. Include diagrams where appropriate to illustrate the series relationships and system architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: HTML Structure and Semantics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the HTML structure and semantic organization of the Mori-universe project. Document the semantic HTML5 structure using tables for data presentation, the tabbed interface pattern with div containers, and the responsive grid layouts. Explain the table-based architecture for displaying literary series data, the phase cards for system evolution visualization, and the evolution tracks for character development analysis. Detail the semantic markup patterns including the use of proper heading hierarchies, table semantics with thead/tbody structure, and the grid-based layout system. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing how the tab system works with showTab() function, how the table data is structured for each series, and how the responsive design adapts to different screen sizes. Document the integration patterns between different HTML files and the modular approach to content organization. Address accessibility considerations including proper ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation support, and screen reader compatibility. Include best practices for maintaining semantic HTML structure and optimizing for SEO and accessibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Navigation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the tabbed navigation system that powers the Mori-universe website. Explain the pure JavaScript implementation of the tab switching mechanism, including the showTab() function and DOM manipulation techniques used to control tab visibility. Document the CSS styling for tab buttons including hover effects, active states, and the gradient color scheme. Detail the responsive design considerations for mobile devices and the flexible tab layout using CSS Flexbox. Include practical examples of how to add new tabs, customize tab styling through CSS variables, and extend the navigation system for additional content sections. Address browser compatibility, accessibility considerations, and performance optimization tips for the JavaScript event handlers.

wiki_catalogs.name: S&M Series (Rationalist Detective)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the S&M Series analysis focused on the core detective work of Shin川创平 and Nishinomiyakō Moe. Explain the series' central theme of applying rigorous logical reasoning to solve seemingly impossible mysteries, with each book serving as a 'Unit Testing' of rationality itself. Document the ten books and their specific technical metaphors including Sandbox Escape (Vol.1), Concurrency Deadlock (Vol.2), Front-end Rendering Fraud (Vol.3), Namespace Pollution (Vol.4), Cache Invalidation (Vol.5), Session Hijacking (Vol.6), Object Cloning (Vol.7), Git Revert (Vol.8), Hash Collision (Vol.9), and Container Escape (Vol.10). Include the evolution of the protagonist's approach from physical hardware bugs to system-level philosophical problems. Provide practical examples of how each mystery demonstrates fundamental computer science concepts like integer overflow, mutex locks, view-model desynchronization, pattern matching, TTL misconfiguration, attention pointer manipulation, deep copy vs shallow copy, time complexity, collision attacks, and container escape. Document the series' role as the logical core of Mori Hiroshi's universe and its relationship to the Root User character.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the Mori-universe project focused on quick onboarding and basic usage. Explain installation and setup instructions for accessing the static HTML files offline, browser compatibility requirements, and offline access capabilities. Document the basic navigation patterns including tab switching functionality, series exploration methods, and interactive feature usage. Include step-by-step guidance for new users to navigate the different series (S&M, V, Shiki, G, X, Century, Sky Crawlers, W, WW, Void Shaper) and understand the color-coded identification system. Provide practical examples demonstrating how to use the tabbed interface, explore series catalogs, and utilize the screenshot functionality. Document the responsive design features and mobile optimization. Address common beginner questions about the literary-to-technical metaphors and cross-referencing system. Include troubleshooting tips for basic functionality and browser compatibility issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: CSS Architecture and Theming System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the CSS architecture and theming system of the Mori-universe project. Document the CSS custom properties system using :root variables for theming, the dark mode color scheme with --bg, --card, and --text variables, and the series-specific color coding system. Explain the implementation of responsive design using CSS media queries targeting 768px breakpoints, the grid-based layout system for phase cards and evolution tracks, and the table styling architecture with sticky headers and hover effects. Detail the CSS variable usage patterns for consistent theming across different series colors, the modular CSS architecture with separate styles for tables, cards, and responsive components. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing how the color-coded series identification works with .series-sm, .series-v classes, how the CSS custom properties enable theme switching, and how the responsive design adapts to mobile devices. Document the print media styles, the backdrop-filter effects for the screenshot button, and the gradient color schemes. Address performance considerations including CSS optimization techniques, the lightweight approach with minimal dependencies, and best practices for maintaining the pure CSS architecture without external frameworks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Interactive Visualizations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the interactive visualization components, focusing on the runtime visualization system for 'Everything Becomes F' and the screenshot functionality. Explain the html2canvas integration for page capture, including the capturePageAsImage() function implementation, canvas configuration options, and image export functionality. Document the CSS styling for the screenshot button including the floating position, backdrop filter effects, and hover animations. Cover the responsive design considerations for visualizations across different screen sizes. Include examples of customizing visualization parameters, handling errors during image capture, and extending the system for additional visualization components. Address browser compatibility issues, performance considerations for large page captures, and security implications of client-side image generation.

wiki_catalogs.name: V Series (Perception and Reality)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the V Series analysis focused on the exploration of perception, reality, and cognitive limitations through the lens of psychological phenomena. Explain how the series uses perceptual psychology and pattern recognition to challenge the reliability of human senses and mental processing. Document the ten books' approach to demonstrating how our brains construct reality through pre-processing, leading to systematic errors in perception. Include the technical metaphors of Pattern Recognition, Symmetry Breaking, and visual deception mapping to computer vision and neural network concepts. Provide practical examples of how each book illustrates the observer effect, cognitive biases, and the fundamental limits of human perception. Document the series' role as the 'Perception Layer' in Mori Hiroshi's system architecture and its relationship to the broader theme of how consciousness interprets reality.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Mori-universe system architecture focused on the technical design patterns and component relationships. Describe the high-level design including the tabbed interface architecture, color-coded series identification system, and interactive visualization components. Document component interactions between the navigation system, content tabs, and visualization engines. Explain the data flow architecture from user input through tab switching to content rendering. Include the implementation details of the pure HTML/CSS/JavaScript architecture with no external dependencies. Document the responsive design system using CSS media queries and the screenshot functionality using html2canvas library. Explain technical decisions including the choice for static files, the tab switching mechanism, and the color-coding system for series identification. Address the modular content organization and progressive disclosure patterns used throughout the interface. Include system context showing how the different series pages integrate with the main overview page.

wiki_catalogs.name: JavaScript Functionality and Interactivity
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the JavaScript functionality and interactivity features of the Mori-universe project. Document the tab switching system implemented with the showTab() function, the event handling patterns for button clicks, and the DOM manipulation techniques for content visibility management. Explain the screenshot functionality using html2canvas library integration, the image capture workflow with loading states and error handling, and the canvas-based image export system. Detail the lightweight JavaScript architecture with no external dependencies, the pure vanilla JS approach for maximum compatibility, and the performance optimization strategies for large datasets in tables. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing how the tab system manages active states, how the screenshot functionality handles user interaction and canvas rendering, and how the responsive design integrates with JavaScript for dynamic content adaptation. Document the browser compatibility considerations, the fallback strategies for unsupported features, and the debugging approaches for JavaScript functionality. Address security considerations including CORS handling, data sanitization for user-generated content, and best practices for maintaining the pure JavaScript approach without introducing vulnerabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cross-Reference System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the cross-reference system that connects literary works across different series and provides color-coded categorization. Explain the series identification system using CSS classes (series-sm, series-v, series-shiki, etc.) and the corresponding tag-based labeling system. Document the color palette implementation through CSS custom properties and how the theming system maintains consistency across different series representations. Detail the table-based cross-reference structure, including the series catalog tables and their responsive design. Cover the series badge system for phase cards and evolution tracks, explaining how different series are visually distinguished and linked. Include examples of adding new series to the cross-reference system, customizing color schemes, and extending the categorization system for additional literary works.

wiki_catalogs.name: Shiki Series (System Architecture)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Shiki Series analysis focused on the four-volume system architecture that serves as the core kernel of Mori Hiroshi's universe. Explain how the series chronicles the compilation of an operating system through the life stages of Akutagawa Shiki, from childhood anomalies through adult system design. Document the technical metaphors of Kernel Source, Lifecycle Management, and State Persistence as they relate to consciousness and identity. Include the detailed analysis of how each season represents a different phase of system development: Spring as the kernel bootstrap, Summer as algorithm optimization, Autumn as architectural design, and Winter as final compilation and persistence. Provide practical examples of the interactive runtime visualization and evolution tracking systems. Document the series' role as the 'Kernel Source' in the overall system architecture and its relationship to the Root User concept.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features and Functionality
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the core features and functionality section covering the main interactive capabilities of the Mori-universe project. Thoroughly explain the tabbed navigation system implementation, interactive visualization components, and cross-reference database functionality. Document the tab switching mechanism using pure JavaScript, the color-coded series identification system, and the screenshot functionality. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing how the navigation system works, how series are categorized and linked, and how interactive visualizations are implemented. Document configuration options including the CSS variables for theming, responsive breakpoints, and JavaScript event handlers. Explain relationships with other components including how the main overview page integrates with series-specific content. Address common issues like browser compatibility and performance optimization. Make content accessible to beginners while providing sufficient technical depth for experienced developers interested in the implementation details.

wiki_catalogs.name: Responsive Design System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the responsive design implementation that ensures optimal viewing across different device sizes and screen orientations. Explain the CSS media query strategy used in the @media (max-width: 768px) breakpoint, including adjustments to typography scaling, spacing modifications, and layout changes for mobile devices. Detail the flexible grid systems using CSS Grid and Flexbox for the evolution tracks and phase cards. Cover the mobile-optimized tab layout and how the navigation adapts for smaller screens. Document the print media styles and how they affect the screenshot button visibility. Include examples of customizing breakpoints, adjusting responsive typography scales, and implementing additional responsive components. Address performance considerations for responsive design, browser compatibility for modern CSS features, and best practices for maintaining visual consistency across different viewport sizes.

wiki_catalogs.name: G Series (Graph Theory and Networks)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the G Series analysis focused on the exploration of relationships, connections, and network structures through the lens of graph theory. Explain how the series challenges traditional notions of identity by questioning whether individuals are better understood as nodes or edges in social networks. Document the eleven books' approach to exploring fundamental questions about the nature of relationships, community formation, and collective intelligence. Include the technical metaphors of Graph Theory, Probability & Statistics, and GNN Thinking as they apply to understanding human connections and social dynamics. Provide practical examples of how each book demonstrates the shift from individual-centric thinking to network-centric understanding. Document the series' role in the 'Network Layer' of the overall system architecture and its relationship to the broader themes of distributed consciousness and social emergence.

wiki_catalogs.name: Literary Series Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the literary series analysis section focused on the detailed examination of Mori Hiroshi's major series and their technical parallels. Explain the purpose of mapping literary concepts to programming/technical principles, the methodology for cross-referencing between series, and the color-coded identification system. Document the implementation details of the series categorization including the ten major series (S&M, V, Shiki, G, X, Century, Sky Crawlers, W, WW, Void Shaper) and their technical metaphors. Include both conceptual overviews for literary analysis and technical details for programmers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including series names, technical concepts, and literary themes. Provide practical examples demonstrating how each series connects to programming principles like sandbox escape, pattern recognition, kernel source, graph theory, recursion, and distributed consciousness. Document the cross-referencing system that links related concepts across different series and the chronological organization of the complete works catalog.

wiki_catalogs.name: X Series (Structural Engineering)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the X Series analysis focused on structural engineering principles applied to literary narrative construction. Explain how the series uses the metaphor of building and maintaining complex structures to explore themes of legacy, maintenance, and architectural evolution. Document the six books' approach to demonstrating the challenges of working with existing systems versus building new ones, using the technical metaphors of Structural Engineering, Reverse Engineering, and Legacy Code Maintenance. Include practical examples of how each book illustrates the concept of Technical Debt and the difficulty of maintaining old systems compared to building new ones. Provide detailed analysis of the relationship between the series' structural metaphors and the broader themes of system evolution and architectural continuity. Document the series' role in connecting the logical core to practical implementation challenges.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technical Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive technical implementation documentation focused on the HTML structure, CSS architecture, and JavaScript functionality of the Mori-universe project. Document the semantic HTML structure using tables for data presentation, the CSS custom properties system for theming, and the JavaScript event handling for tab switching. Explain the implementation details of the responsive design using CSS media queries, the screenshot functionality using html2canvas library, and the color-coded series identification system. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing how the tabbed interface works, how the CSS variables enable theming, and how the JavaScript manages content visibility. Document the integration patterns between different HTML files and the modular approach to content organization. Address performance considerations including the lightweight architecture with no external dependencies, optimization tips for large datasets in tables, and best practices for maintaining the pure HTML/CSS/JavaScript approach. Include troubleshooting guidance for common implementation issues and browser compatibility considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Century Series (Immortality)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Century Series analysis focused on the exploration of immortality, stagnation, and the paradoxes of eternal existence. Explain how the series uses the technical metaphors of Data Persistence, Cache Stale, and System Stagnation to explore the philosophical implications of living forever. Document the three books' approach to questioning whether immortality is truly desirable when it implies the cessation of meaningful change and growth. Include practical examples of how each book demonstrates the concept that eternal life might be equivalent to system stagnation. Provide detailed analysis of the relationship between the series' themes and the broader narrative arc toward distributed consciousness. Document the series' role as part of the 'Endgame' trilogy alongside W and WW series, exploring the ultimate fate of consciousness in an immortal state.

wiki_catalogs.name: Advanced Topics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for advanced topics focused on the technical documentation and interdisciplinary analysis methods used in the Mori-universe project. Explain the advanced system architecture concepts including the mapping between literary themes and computer science principles, the methodology for cross-disciplinary analysis combining literature and technology studies. Document the implementation details of the technical documentation including the F——大型Unix系统讲义 content and its relevance to the overall system architecture. Include both conceptual overviews for researchers and technical details for developers interested in systems thinking. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including technical concepts, literary analysis frameworks, and cross-referencing methodologies. Provide practical examples demonstrating advanced analysis techniques, the integration of multiple analytical perspectives, and the extension mechanisms for adding new content. Document the advanced features like the runtime visualization system and its relationship to the broader system architecture. Address expert-level usage patterns including how to extend the cross-reference system, customize the visualization components, and integrate new series content.

wiki_catalogs.name: Sky Crawlers (Infinite Loops)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Sky Crawlers series analysis focused on the exploration of infinite loops, recursive functions without base cases, and the philosophical implications of processes without termination. Explain how the series uses the metaphor of Kildren trapped in an endless cycle to explore fundamental questions about the meaning of existence without purpose or endpoint. Document the six books' approach to demonstrating the concept that some processes, while logically sound, may lack meaningful direction without proper termination conditions. Include practical examples of how each book illustrates the relationship between recursion, state machines, and infinite loops. Provide detailed analysis of the series' role as a parallel branch in the overall narrative, exploring themes that complement but diverge from the main logical progression. Document the series' connection to the broader themes of consciousness, identity, and the search for meaning in an unending existence.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integration and Extension
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for integration and extension focused on extending the Mori-universe project with new series content and customizing existing functionality. Explain the implementation details of adding new series including the tab-based navigation system modifications, color-coded identification system integration, and cross-reference database updates. Document the extension mechanisms including how to add new series pages, customize the visualization components, and extend the cross-reference capabilities. Include concrete examples from the actual codebase showing how to modify the tab switching logic, update the color scheme using CSS variables, and integrate new content into the existing architecture. Document the integration points including the series-to-technology mapping system, chronological cross-referencing patterns, and character network analysis capabilities. Address the customization options including theme modifications, layout changes, and content additions. Provide practical guidance for developers wanting to extend the project including file organization patterns, naming conventions, and maintenance considerations. Include troubleshooting guidance for common extension scenarios and best practices for maintaining the pure HTML/CSS/JavaScript architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: W Series (Artificial Life)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the W Series analysis focused on artificial life, artificial consciousness, and the blurring of boundaries between human and artificial entities. Explain how the series uses the metaphor of Walk-alone (沃克隆) to explore the philosophical implications of artificial beings achieving human-like qualities. Document the ten books' approach to challenging traditional definitions of humanity through the lens of Black-box Fitting, Artificial Life, and Consciousness Virtualization. Include practical examples of how each book demonstrates the difficulty of distinguishing between authentic human consciousness and sophisticated artificial versions. Provide detailed analysis of the series' role in the 'Endgame' trilogy, exploring the implications of artificial life forms achieving self-awareness. Document the series' connection to the broader themes of identity, consciousness, and the future of human-AI relationships.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and FAQ content focused on common issues and solutions for the Mori-universe project. Document common problems including browser compatibility issues, performance optimization tips, and responsive design troubleshooting. Explain solutions for tab switching problems, screenshot functionality failures, and cross-reference system issues. Include practical examples demonstrating how to resolve common technical issues, optimize performance for large datasets, and address browser-specific problems. Document the troubleshooting steps for different scenarios including mobile device compatibility, offline access issues, and content loading problems. Address frequently asked questions about the project's purpose, technical implementation choices, and usage patterns. Provide guidance for users experiencing difficulties with the interactive features, navigation system, or visualizations. Include solutions for common user scenarios like printing or saving content, navigating between series, and understanding the color-coded identification system. Document best practices for maintaining the project and avoiding common pitfalls in the pure HTML/CSS/JavaScript architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: WW Series (Distributed Consciousness)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the WW Series analysis focused on distributed consciousness, decentralized identity, and global emergence of collective awareness. Explain how the series explores the concept of consciousness being distributed across multiple nodes while maintaining individual identity, using the technical metaphors of Distributed Consciousness, Decentralization, and Global Emergence. Document the eight+ volumes' approach to questioning whether a person remains the same when their consciousness is spread across multiple locations or systems. Include practical examples of how each book demonstrates the challenges of Identity Consensus in distributed systems. Provide detailed analysis of the series' role as the most cutting-edge exploration of consciousness in Mori Hiroshi's universe, representing the ultimate evolution beyond individual or even distributed forms. Document the series' connection to contemporary discussions about cloud computing, AI consciousness, and the future of human identity in networked societies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Void Shaper Series (Bushido Philosophy)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the Void Shaper series analysis focused on the exploration of introspective algorithms, human nature, and Bushido philosophy through the metaphor of Void Shaper (虚空塑形者). Explain how the series uses the concept of shaping emptiness to explore the philosophical journey of self-discovery and personal cultivation. Document the five books' approach to demonstrating how the process of examining one's inner void leads to greater self-awareness and understanding of human nature. Include practical examples of how each book illustrates the Introspection Algorithm and Human Nature Exploration as they relate to traditional martial arts philosophy and modern psychological self-reflection. Provide detailed analysis of the series' role as the philosophical foundation for understanding consciousness, contrasting with the more technical approaches of other series. Document the series' connection to the broader themes of personal growth, self-mastery, and the pursuit of enlightenment through systematic self-examination.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Mori-Universe Project Overview

## Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
Mori-Universe is a comprehensive digital archive and analytical framework that maps the interconnected universe of Japanese author Mori Hiroshi's literary works. The project serves as both a technical documentation platform and an educational resource for understanding the deep mathematical, philosophical, and systems-thinking foundations embedded throughout his extensive bibliography.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Archival Preservation**: Create a comprehensive digital repository of Mori Hiroshi's complete works and related materials
- **Systems Thinking Education**: Bridge literature and computer science through detailed analysis of algorithmic themes
- **Interdisciplinary Analysis**: Connect literary concepts with technical architecture and philosophical frameworks
- **Educational Resource**: Provide structured learning pathways for understanding complex narrative architectures

### Target Audience
- Computer scientists and engineers interested in literary algorithms
- Students studying systems thinking and computational philosophy
- Literary scholars exploring technical themes in modern fiction
- Developers seeking inspiration from narrative architecture patterns

## Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Mori-Universe Platform] --> B[HTML Documentation System]
    A --> C[Interactive Visualization Tools]
    A --> D[Cross-Reference Database]
    
    B --> E[Series Overview Pages]
    B --> F[System Architecture Maps]
    B --> G[Complete Works Catalogs]
    
    C --> H[Timeline Visualizations]
    C --> I[Evolution Tracks]
    C --> J[Concept Mapping]
    
    D --> K[Character Relationships]
    D --> L[Thematic Connections]
    D --> M[Technical References]
```

### Design Patterns
- **Modular Documentation**: Each literary series gets dedicated visualization pages
- **Progressive Disclosure**: Complex systems revealed through layered tabs and interactive elements
- **Cross-Referencing**: Direct links between related concepts across different series
- **Visual Metaphors**: Programming concepts mapped to literary themes

### System Relationships

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Input] --> B[Navigation System]
    B --> C[Content Selection]
    C --> D[Visualization Engine]
    D --> E[Interactive Display]
    
    F[Series Data] --> G[Archival Database]
    G --> H[Search Index]
    H --> I[Cross-Reference Engine]
    
    I --> J[Related Content Discovery]
    J --> K[Concept Mapping]
    K --> L[Learning Path Generation]
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **mori_system_overview.html** - Primary navigation hub for the entire Mori universe
- **shiki/shiki_system_architecture.html** - Deep dive into the Shiki series system architecture
- **mori_complete_works.html** - Comprehensive catalog of all published works
- **shiki/everything_becomes_f_runtime.html** - Interactive runtime log visualization

### Core Modules
- **Series Architecture Module**: Maps each literary series to corresponding technical concepts
- **Evolution Tracking System**: Visualizes character and concept development over time
- **Cross-Reference Engine**: Links related themes, characters, and technical concepts
- **Interactive Visualization Toolkit**: Provides dynamic representations of complex systems

### Configuration Approach
- **Tab-based Navigation**: Clean separation of different content types
- **Color-coded Series Identification**: Visual distinction between different literary universes
- **Responsive Design**: Optimized for both desktop and mobile viewing
- **Progressive Enhancement**: Basic HTML content with JavaScript enhancement

### External Dependencies
- **Pure HTML/CSS/JavaScript**: No external libraries, ensuring maximum compatibility
- **Local File System**: All content stored locally for offline access
- **Browser Compatibility**: Designed for modern web browsers with graceful degradation

### Integration Points
- **Series-to-Technology Mapping**: Direct correlation between literary concepts and programming paradigms
- **Chronological Cross-referencing**: Timeline-based relationships between works
- **Character Network Analysis**: Social graph visualization of literary relationships
- **Conceptual Thesaurus**: Semantic mapping between literary themes and technical concepts

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Complete Works] --> B[Series Catalogs]
    B --> C[System Architecture]
    B --> D[Evolution Tracks]
    
    E[Individual Series] --> F[Core Concepts]
    F --> G[Technical Parallels]
    F --> H[Philosophical Themes]
    
    I[Interactive Tools] --> J[Visualization Engine]
    J --> K[Dynamic Content Loading]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview
- **Comprehensive Literary Analysis**: Detailed mapping of Mori Hiroshi's interconnected narrative universe
- **Technical Concept Integration**: Bridging between literary themes and computer science principles
- **Interactive Learning Experience**: Engaging visualizations that make complex systems accessible
- **Cross-Platform Accessibility**: Works across different devices and browsers

### Implementation Highlights
- **mori_system_overview.html** - Central hub with tabbed interface for different analytical perspectives
- **shiki/shiki_system_architecture.html** - Deep technical analysis of the Shiki series' system design
- **shiki/everything_becomes_f_runtime.html** - Interactive timeline visualization of the "Everything Becomes F" narrative
- **mori_complete_works.html** - Complete catalog with color-coded series identification

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Discovery
    Discovery --> SeriesSelection: Choose Literary Focus
    SeriesSelection --> ConceptMapping: Map Technical Concepts
    ConceptMapping --> Visualization: Render Interactive Elements
    Visualization --> Analysis: Deep Dive Analysis
    Analysis --> [*]: Knowledge Integration
    
    SeriesSelection --> CrossReference: Find Related Works
    CrossReference --> ConceptMapping
```

The Mori-Universe project represents a unique intersection of literature and technology, offering both entertainment and educational value through its innovative approach to literary analysis and systems thinking visualization.

**Sources:**
- [mori_system_overview.html](mori_system_overview.html)
- [shiki_system_architecture.html](shiki/shiki_system_architecture.html)
- [everything_becomes_f_runtime.html](shiki/everything_becomes_f_runtime.html)
- [mori_complete_works.html](mori_complete_works.html)
- [F——大型Unix系统讲义](F——大型Unix系统讲义)

</blog>
